note: source.url https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32024R1735 resolves to Regulation (EU) 2024/1735 (Net-Zero Industry Act) — confirmed official EUR-Lex. EN IEC 61730-1/-2:2018+A11 are valid harmonised standards under LVD (A11 amendment published 2022 in some listings, 2020 in others; both pre-date the 2023 IEC edition which is not yet the harmonised EN version). WEEE Category 4 (large equipment) claim for PV panels is correct per post-2018 open-scope Annex III. Mandatory status is accurate. NZIA Art. 26 correctly marked [UNVERIFIED/not a hard market-access gate]. Minor inconsistency: this row cites EN IEC 61730-1:2018+A11:2022 while pv-module-safety-qualification cites +A11:2020 — cross-check needed, but substantive content is sound. RoHS scope note in summary ('PV modules currently not listed in Annex I as in-scope equipment categories') is technically correct but understates the position: PV panels are positively excluded from RoHS scope under Article 2(4)(h) of 2011/65/EU — they are entirely out of scope, not merely 'not listed in Annex I'. This framing error is less severe here than in peroveu-rohs-001 but should be corrected.

source.url https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/61032 returns HTTP 404 — dead link. The correct IEC webstore URL for IEC 61215-1:2021 is https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/61345 (confirmed via IEC webstore search). Separately, IEC TS 63209-2 is dated 2022 (not 2021 as stated in the standards_or_laws list in this row); IEC TS 63209-2:2022 covers polymeric component materials, not 'bill of materials' as described — the scope description is also imprecise. IEC TS 63209-1:2021 date is correct. IEC 61215-1:2021 and 61215-2:2021 existence confirmed. IEC 61853 series dates (61853-1:2011 / 61853-2:2016 / 61853-3:2018 / 61853-4:2018) appear correct based on known IEC publication history. Mandatory status is accurate. Gap analysis is substantively accurate regarding perovskite reliability challenges under IEC 61215. Fix: update source.url to pub/61345; correct IEC TS 63209-2 year to 2022 and scope description.

source.url https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2012/19/oj/eng resolves to WEEE Directive 2012/19/EU on EUR-Lex — confirmed official. CRITICAL CATEGORY ERROR: fragment states 'Annex I Category 4: Large Equipment; and Annex III, Category 11: Photovoltaic panels'. WEEE 2012/19/EU does NOT have a 'Category 11'. Under the open-scope regime (effective 15 August 2018), Annex III has only 6 categories; photovoltaic panels fall under Category 4 (Large Equipment, any external dimension >50 cm). For reporting purposes, sub-category '4b: Photovoltaic panels' was formalised in Commission Implementing Decision 2019/2193 — but there is no 'Category 11' in WEEE. 'Category 11' is a category in RoHS Annex I (Other EEE not covered by other categories), not WEEE. This is a cross-contamination error between the two directives. The substantive compliance obligations described (producer registration per MS, authorised representative, crossed-out bin marking) are correct. Mandatory status is correct. Fix: remove 'Annex III, Category 11' reference; describe PV panels as Category 4 (sub-category 4b) under post-2018 open scope.

source.url https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2011/65/oj/eng resolves to Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS II) on EUR-Lex — confirmed official. Standards cited (Annex II Pb MCV, Annex III exemptions, Directive 2015/863/EU, EN IEC 63000:2018) are real and accurately described. CRITICAL FACTUAL ERROR IN PREMISE: The fragment states PV panels 'were brought into RoHS scope under Category 11 (Other EEE not covered by other categories) by Directive 2011/65/EU as amended.' This is INCORRECT. Photovoltaic panels intended for permanent installation by professionals are EXCLUDED from the scope of Directive 2011/65/EU under Article 2(4)(h). This is an explicit scope exclusion — PV panels are entirely outside RoHS, not merely unexempted. Since 2013 (confirmed by EU industry sources including PV Cycle and ComplianceGate) PV panels have been excluded from RoHS at the start-of-life phase as part of the legislative trade-off that brought them into WEEE scope for end-of-life. Consequence: the entire risk framing — that lead-perovskite modules face 'probable RoHS non-compliance' absent an Annex III exemption — is likely incorrect for standard professionally-installed PV modules. The 0.1% Pb MCV does not apply. No Annex III exemption is needed or sought for this product category. The REACH SVHC observation (Article 33 notification for lead compounds in articles) is separately valid and correctly noted. mandatory_status claim 'Mandatory — RoHS II is a CE marking prerequisite; Pb MCV 0.1% w/w applies' is INCORRECT for PV panels. The CN equivalent section (GB/T 26572 vs RoHS) is technically accurate as a comparison of the standards but the EU-side premise is wrong. Note: a small segment of the PV value chain (inverters, junction boxes with electronics) could be EEE in scope — but the absorber module itself is excluded. This row requires substantial correction of its core RoHS-scope claim before publication.

source.url https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32014L0035 resolves to LVD 2014/35/EU on EUR-Lex — confirmed official. EN IEC 61730-1:2018+A11:2020 and EN IEC 61730-2:2018+A11:2020 are confirmed harmonised standards under the LVD (listed on EU harmonised standards register). Note: IEC published IEC 61730-1:2023 (Edition 3.0, September 2023) and IEC 61730-2:2023 as new international editions; however, the EN harmonised versions under LVD are still based on the 2018 editions with amendments. The fragment correctly uses the 'EN IEC' prefix indicating harmonised European adoption of the IEC standard, which is appropriate for LVD CE-marking purposes. Note inconsistency with peroveu-market-01 which cites A11:2022 vs this row's A11:2020 — one amendment year is likely wrong; the 2020 amendment year in this row appears more consistent with CE marking assistant records. Mandatory status ('Mandatory for CE marking under LVD') is correct. Gap analysis noting absence of perovskite-specific harmonised sub-standard is accurate as of 2026.

source.url https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/64736 returns HTTP 404 — dead link. The correct IEC webstore URL for IEC 61215-2:2021 is https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/61350 (confirmed via IEC webstore search). Separately, IEC TS 63209-2 is cited as publication year 2021 but the correct year is 2022; also IEC TS 63209-2:2022 covers 'polymeric component materials', not 'bill of materials' as described in the UNVERIFIED note — this scope description is inaccurate. IEC TS 63209-1:2021 (Part 1: Modules) year is correct. EN IEC 61215-1:2021 and 61215-2:2021 confirmed. The note '[UNVERIFIED: exact publication year and scope]' on IEC TS 63209-2 correctly flags uncertainty. Mandatory status is accurate. Fix: update source.url to pub/61350; correct IEC TS 63209-2 year to 2022 and part description to 'polymeric component materials'.
